output.final_deliverable:
Evidence is missing because no search/visit results were provided in the merged results.

What I need (from official Docker documentation pages) to complete the extraction:
1) A brief definition of what Docker is (as stated on docs.docker.com).
2) The latest stable Docker version number (as stated on an official Docker Docs release notes / versioning page).
3) Three key features/capabilities (as stated on official Docker Docs pages—e.g., containers, image building, Compose, networking, volumes, etc.).
4) The official installation guide link (from docs.docker.com/get-docker/ or equivalent).

No URLs were visited and no official documentation content was included in the merged results, so I cannot cite or extract the requested items yet.

results.title: Engine v29 | Docker Docs
results.description: Answers are generated based on the documentation. ... This page describes the latest changes, additions, known issues, and fixes for Docker Engine version 29. ... Deprecated and removed features, see Deprecated Engine Features.

results.title: Release notes | Docker Docs
results.description: If you’re seeing a security popup about a malware on com.docker.vmnetd or com.docker.socket, follow the steps documented in docker/for-mac#7527. ... You can now perform key operations such as starting, stopping, restarting, and checking the status of Docker Desktop directly from the command line (Beta). The AI Catalog in Docker Hub is available directly through Docker Desktop. ... The default disk usage limit for Docker Engine in new installations is now 1TB.

results.description: These binaries are statically linked and you can use them on any Linux distribution. Docker Engine has two types of update channels, stable and test: The stable channel gives you the latest versions released for general availability.
